section_name: Prototype Pollution: recursive merge with user keys
description: Recursive merge/extend/defaults operation — if keys come from user input (req.body, JSON.parse), __proto__ or constructor.prototype paths can pollute Object.prototype

section_name: Command Injection: child_process with user input
description: child_process exec/spawn — if command string includes user input without escaping, OS command injection is possible. execFile with array args is safer.

section_name: Path Traversal: fs ops with user-controlled path
description: File system operation — if the path argument derives from user input (req.params, req.query) without path normalization and prefix check, directory traversal (../) is possible

section_name: SSRF: HTTP request with user-controlled URL
description: Outbound HTTP request — if the URL/host comes from user input without private-IP blocking or URL allowlist, SSRF to internal services or cloud metadata is possible
Dynamic RegExp construction — if the pattern comes from user input, catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) is possible. Also check static regexes with nested quantifiers.

section_name: SQL Injection: raw query with string interpolation
description: Raw SQL query construction — if user input is interpolated into the query string instead of using parameterized queries/placeholders, SQL injection is possible

section_name: Eval Injection: eval/Function with user input
description: Code evaluation — if the evaluated string/template includes user input, arbitrary code execution is possible
JWT verification — if the algorithm is not pinned (taken from token header), alg:none or RSA→HMAC confusion attacks are possible
MongoDB query with user-controlled filter — if req.body/query is passed directly, operators like $gt, $where, $regex can be injected for auth bypass or data extraction

section_name: XXE: XML parsing without entity restriction
description: XML parsing — if external entities are not disabled, XXE attacks can read local files or perform SSRF. Check parser configuration for entity restrictions.
